TextBlock.Text: COASTWATCHER NET LOMBOK VIA RAN DARWIN NT AU

Grid.TextBlock: CINCPACFLT PEARL HARBOR HI HQ AIR COMMAND RAAF GLENBROOK NSW AU INDONESIAN NAVAL LIAISON DARWIN NT AU

StackPanel.TextBlock:
SECRET
BT
1. (S) SUMMARY: Allied coastwatcher sources report Soviet and allied forces consolidating positions around Denpasar airfield and Lombok Strait. Enemy activity indicates preparation to hold the strait against surface penetration.
2. (S) SIGHTINGS: Two destroyer-sized combatants were observed north of Lombok Strait during last light 19AUG. Smaller fast patrol craft reported operating in pairs inside the strait. Multiple armed auxiliaries and landing craft remain near Bali.
3. (S) SHORE DEFENSES: Observers report radar operation and heavy gun positions near Denpasar airfield. At least one probable coastal missile battery has displaced twice in the last 48 hours. AAA emplacements reported near the airfield as well.
4. (S) AIR ACTIVITY: Short-takeoff jet aircraft observed operating from Bali airfield in limited numbers. Aircraft remained local and did not pursue civilian traffic beyond visual range. Rotary-wing patrols continue along the coast by tandem rotor helos outfitted with anti-tank weapons and bombs, likely Ka-29 type from IVAN ROGOV or ROPUCHA class LST photographed in the harbor.
5. (S) CIVILIAN TRAFFIC: Fishing boats remain in Lombok Strait despite repeated warnings. Some vessels may be under enemy observation or coercion. Positive identification is required before engagement.
6. (S) ASSESSMENT: Enemy intends to contest Allied passage to delay relief of Indonesia. Sources report no confirmed submarine activity in immediate area.
SIGNED: CAPTAIN M. S. SULLIVAN, RAN
ROYAL AUSTRALIAN NAVY LIAISON
BT
SECRET DECL OADR
